Google Chrome.risk: Outdated web browsers are a primary entry point for malware and phishing attacks.
Google Chrome.remediation:
1. Open Chrome.
2. Click the three dots in the top-right corner.
3. Go to 'Help' -> 'About Google Chrome'.
4. Chrome will automatically find and apply the latest update. Relaunch when prompted.
walkthrough: Click the three dots (⋮) in the top-right corner
Wait for Chrome to check for updates and automatically install them
Relaunch Chrome to complete the update

Mozilla Firefox.risk: Outdated web browsers are a primary entry point for malware and phishing attacks.
Mozilla Firefox.remediation:
1. Open Firefox.
2. Click the menu button (three horizontal lines) in the top-right corner.
3. Select 'Help' → 'About Firefox'.
4. Firefox will automatically check for and download updates.
5. Restart Firefox to apply the update.
walkthrough: Click the menu button (☰) in the top-right corner
Allow Firefox to check for and install updates

Adobe Acrobat Reader DC.risk: Vulnerabilities in document readers can allow attackers to execute malicious code on your system.
Adobe Acrobat Reader DC.remediation:
1. Open Adobe Acrobat Reader DC.
2. Click 'Help' in the top menu bar.
3. Select 'Check for Updates'.
4. If updates are available, follow the prompts to install them.
5. Restart the application once the update completes.
walkthrough: Click 'Help' in the top menu bar

Java 8 Update 371.risk: Older versions of Java have numerous security flaws that are actively exploited.
Java 8 Update 371.remediation:
1. Visit the official Oracle Java download page.
2. Download the latest version of Java appropriate for your operating system.
3. Close all applications using Java.
4. Run the installer and follow on-screen instructions.
5. Verify the update by running 'java -version' in a command prompt or terminal.
walkthrough: Go to the official Oracle Java website
Download the latest version for your OS
Install the downloaded version by following prompts
